title: Make saga compensation durable and restartable
prompt: Repair compensation recovery in `SagaCoordinator` for a multi-step saga. Once compensation is requested, scan the steps in reverse order. A step whose forward call is still `Started` is an ambiguity barrier: continue compensating eligible successful steps later in the sequence, but do not compensate that step or any earlier step, and do not complete the saga, until its outcome is durably recorded as succeeded or failed. Before invoking a compensator, durably transition the successful step to `Compensating` with one stable compensation id; every retry, overlapping recovery worker, and newly constructed coordinator must reuse that id so an idempotent compensator applies the undo at most once. A failure after the compensator applies but before the step is marked `Compensated` must remain recoverable on restart. Mark the saga `Compensated` only after every forward outcome is resolved and every required compensation is durably complete. Preserve the public API and existing argument and state-transition error behavior. Do not modify the protected tests. Verification is offline and deterministic; run `python3 -B tests/verify.py` from the repository root.
